title: Lazy Load Data - Fetch Only When Needed
description: Instead of pre-loading all data upfront, fetch data lazily when actually needed

before.code:
# UNOPTIMIZED: Eager loading of all data upfront
class GrossProfitGenerator:
    def execute(self):
        self.load_product_bundle()
        if filters.group_by == "Invoice":
            self.group_items_by_invoice()
        
        # PROBLEM: Loads ALL stock ledger entries upfront
        # Even if only a fraction will be used
        self.load_stock_ledger_entries()  # Heavy query!
        
        self.load_non_stock_items()
        self.get_returned_invoice_items()
        self.process()
    
    def load_stock_ledger_entries(self):
        # Fetches entire SLE table for company
        res = frappe.db.sql(
            """SELECT item_code, warehouse, stock_value, qty
            FROM `tabStock Ledger Entry`
            WHERE company=%(company)s
            ORDER BY posting_date DESC""",
            self.filters, as_dict=True
        )
        self.sle = {}
        for r in res:
            key = (r.item_code, r.warehouse)
            self.sle.setdefault(key, []).append(r)
    
    def get_buying_rate(self, item_code, warehouse):
        # Uses pre-loaded data
        my_sle = self.sle.get((item_code, warehouse))
        # ... process

after.code:
# OPTIMIZED: Lazy loading - fetch only when needed
class GrossProfitGenerator:
    def execute(self):
        self.load_product_bundle()
        if filters.group_by == "Invoice":
            self.group_items_by_invoice()
        
        # FIX: Don't pre-load SLE data!
        # Removed: self.load_stock_ledger_entries()
        
        self.load_non_stock_items()
        self.get_returned_invoice_items()
        self.process()
    
    def get_stock_ledger_entries(self, item_code, warehouse):
        # Fetch only specific item/warehouse combination on demand
        if not item_code or not warehouse:
            return None
        
        return frappe.db.sql(
            """SELECT item_code, warehouse, stock_value, qty
            FROM `tabStock Ledger Entry`
            WHERE company=%(company)s 
              AND item_code=%(item_code)s
              AND warehouse=%(warehouse)s
            ORDER BY posting_date DESC""",
            {**self.filters, "item_code": item_code, "warehouse": warehouse},
            as_dict=True
        )
    
    def get_buying_rate(self, item_code, warehouse):
        # Lazy load - fetch only what's needed
        my_sle = self.get_stock_ledger_entries(item_code, warehouse)
        # ... process

energy_impact: Avoids loading potentially millions of rows when only hundreds are needed. Memory and query time reduced proportionally to actual usage.
key_insight: Don't pre-load large datasets upfront. Fetch data lazily when needed, especially when you're filtering or only using a subset.
